metadata.description: Standards bypass configuration for this branch

bypass.reason: setup_handlers() is a wiring function that registers event handlers, not an event handler itself. It does not handle any event — it calls trigger.on() to wire handle_{event} callbacks.
Intentional: handler callback runs inside event bus fire(). Logging here would cause infinite recursion (logger fires events via trigger).
Meta-logging helper: _log_warning() writes directly to file. Its own except block cannot log — you cannot log a failure to log.
Meta-logging helper: _log_warning() writes directly to file. Its own except block cannot log — you cannot log a failure to log.

Meta-logging helper: _log_warning() writes directly to file. Its own except block cannot log — you cannot log a failure to log.
log_watcher imports error_registry.report() for Medic v2 dedup. This is a deliberate handler-to-handler dependency — the registry IS the dedup backend.
error_reporter imports error_registry for tracking and ai_mail for email delivery. These are its core dependencies — it's the dispatch endpoint of the error pipeline.
Watcher imports trigger module to fire events. Handler→module import needed because watcher operates as standalone watchdog callback.
error_detected handler imports error_registry to query error details during dispatch. Core integration point for the error pipeline.
Startup handler imports memory rollover module to trigger memory checks on startup events. Cross-branch integration via modules layer public API.
Meta-logging helper _log_warning() — cannot log a failure to log. Same pattern as silent_catch bypass.
Deferred event queue handler execution — logging would cause infinite recursion (logger fires events via trigger). Same as silent_catch bypass.
Meta-logging helper _log_warning() — cannot log a failure to log. Same pattern as silent_catch bypass.

Error registry at 870 lines — core dedup engine with fingerprinting, circuit breaker, backoff, and CLI. Splitting would break cohesion of the error tracking lifecycle.
Log watcher at 782 lines — watchdog event handler with position tracking, dedup, and medic integration. Splitting would break the file monitoring pipeline.
error_reporter imports trigger bus to fire error_detected events after reporting. Handler must access event bus to complete the error pipeline.
Log watcher imports trigger bus to fire error_logged/warning_logged events. Watchdog callback must access event bus to bridge file monitoring to event system.
error_detected imports error_registry (line 76) and medic_state — core pipeline dependencies for dispatch gating. Already bypassed under handlers standard.
Registry handler imports trigger module to wire event handlers via trigger.on(). The wiring function must reference the event bus.
module_name parameter is passed by the event bus caller, not auto-detected. Event handlers receive context from fire() data, not from inspect.stack().
module_name parameter is passed by the event bus caller, not auto-detected. Event handlers receive context from fire() data, not from inspect.stack().
Registry wiring function imports trigger module to call trigger.on(). Must access the event bus to register handlers — this IS its purpose.
Local variables inside functions, not module-level constants. Checker false positive on function-scoped assignments.
Local variables inside report_error() function, not module-level constants. Checker false positive on function-scoped assignments.
Local variables inside handle_memory_threshold_exceeded(), not module-level constants. Checker false positive on function-scoped assignments.
Local variables inside handle_error_logged(), not module-level constants. Checker false positive on function-scoped assignments.
Local variables inside handle_error_detected(), not module-level constants. Checker false positive on function-scoped assignments.
Local variables inside startup handler functions, not module-level constants. Checker false positive on function-scoped assignments.
config.py cannot import prax logger — circular dependency. Uses _log_warning() file-based pattern instead. Comment on line 26 documents this.
JSON handler framework utilities — part of the json_structure standard infrastructure. Available for future use by modules that need JSON validation and metrics.

notes.usage: Add entries to 'bypass' list to exclude specific violations

example.reason: Circular dependency - logger cannot import CLI

fields.standard: Standard name: cli, imports, naming, etc. (required)
fields.lines: Optional - specific line numbers to bypass
